## 📋 6 measurement Commands Found

## **1. POWer:ADDNew**

**Description:** This command adds the specified power measurement number. The power measurement

**Set:** `POWer:ADDNew  “POWER<x>”` | **Query:** `POWer:ADDNew?`

**Type:** both | **Families:** MSO2, MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, MSO7 | **Group:** Power

---

## **2. POWer:POWer<x>:TYPe**

**Description:** This command sets or queries the measurement type of the specified power measure

**Set:** `POWer:POWer<x>:TYPe <NR1>` | **Query:** `PPOWer:POWer<x>:TYPe  <Measurement  Type> OWer:POWer<x>:TYPe?`

**Type:** both | **Families:** MSO2, MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, MSO7 | **Group:** Power

---

**Description:** This command sets or queries the voltage source for SOA measurement of the speci

**Type:** both | **Families:** MSO2, MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, MSO7 | **Group:** Power

**Type:** both | **Families:** MSO2, MSO4, MSO5, MSO6, MSO7 | **Group:** Power

**Description:** This command sets or queries the source type for the harmonics measurement of th

## **6. POWer:POWer<x>:HARMONICS:STANDard**

**Description:** This command sets or queries the test mode for harmonics measurement of the spec

**Set:** `POWer:POWer<x>:HARMONICS:STANDard  {NONe  |IEC  |MIL  |AM14  |DO160  |CUSTOM}` | **Query:** `POWer:POWer<x>:HARMONICS:STANDard?`
